name: AVRT - Orthodromic (WPW)
identifyBy: Narrow QRS tachycardia (no delta wave - AV node conducts antegrade)
Retrograde (inverted) P waves visible AFTER the QRS - short RP interval (RP' <70ms)
P waves negative in inferior leads (II, III, aVF) - retrograde atrial activation
Distinguished from AVNRT: P wave visible AFTER QRS with RP' <70ms (in AVNRT, P is buried in or just at end of QRS)
History of WPW (delta wave in sinus rhythm) or pre-excitation pattern on baseline ECG

measurements.pWaves: Retrograde - inverted in II/III/aVF, visible just after QRS
measurements.prInterval: Short RP' <70ms (P after QRS); long P'R to next beat

management: Vagal manoeuvres first: Valsalva, carotid sinus massage, diving reflex (cold water face immersion)
Adenosine 6mg IV rapid bolus: blocks AV node, terminates reentry circuit (effective in orthodromic AVRT)
If adenosine fails: adenosine 12mg IV x1 repeat
Verapamil 5–10mg IV (slow): alternative if adenosine unavailable; CONTRAINDICATED in pre-excited AF
Long-term: accessory pathway ablation (catheter ablation) - highly effective, curative
Refer cardiology: electrophysiology study (EPS) and ablation recommended for all symptomatic WPW
pearls: Orthodromic = AV node antegrade, accessory pathway retrograde - narrow QRS (no delta wave)
Antidromic (opposite) = accessory pathway antegrade, AV node retrograde - WIDE QRS with delta wave
AVRT vs AVNRT: RP' interval is key - AVRT has visible retrograde P after QRS; AVNRT hides P in QRS
Adenosine is SAFE in orthodromic AVRT - it terminates the reentry circuit via AV node block
DO NOT give adenosine/verapamil in PRE-EXCITED AF (very fast irregular wide QRS) - may cause VF
The accessory pathway in WPW can conduct in both directions - but most tachycardias are orthodromic (80–90%)
differentials: AVNRT (P wave buried in or at terminal portion of QRS - shorter RP' and different P wave location)
Sinus tachycardia (upright P waves, rate <150 bpm usually, gradual onset)
Atrial flutter with 2:1 block (regular ~150bpm, sawtooth P waves in inferior leads)
Antidromic AVRT (wide QRS, delta wave visible - looks like VT)
